gene_description: Solute Carrier Family 6 Member 3 (DAT1) — encodes the dopamine transporter, the primary mechanism for dopamine reuptake from the synapse and main target of stimulant medications used in ADHD.

source.title: Response to methylphenidate in adults with ADHD is associated with a polymorphism in SLC6A3 (DAT1)
source.finding: The rs27072 T allele in DAT1 3'-UTR was associated with altered dopamine transporter expression and reduced methylphenidate response in ADHD adults (p=0.009), with a 2.1-fold effect on mRNA expression levels.

TT.effect: Homozygous for the T allele, associated with significantly altered dopamine transporter expression. Individuals may have reduced clinical response to methylphenidate due to changes in dopamine reuptake dynamics. Studies show the T allele affects DAT mRNA expression and translation efficiency, potentially leading to suboptimal stimulant efficacy.
TT.recommendation: Consider dose optimization or alternative stimulant formulations. If methylphenidate response is inadequate, evaluate amphetamine-based alternatives or non-stimulant options such as atomoxetine or guanfacine.

TC.effect: Heterozygous carrier of the T allele with one copy of the reference C allele. May show partially reduced methylphenidate efficacy compared to CC homozygotes. The effect on dopamine transporter expression is intermediate.
TC.recommendation: Standard stimulant dosing is typically appropriate. Monitor clinical response closely during titration and adjust dose based on symptom improvement and tolerability.

CT.effect: Heterozygous carrier of the T allele with one copy of the reference C allele. May show partially reduced methylphenidate efficacy compared to CC homozygotes. The effect on dopamine transporter expression is intermediate.
CT.recommendation: Standard stimulant dosing is typically appropriate. Monitor clinical response closely during titration and adjust dose based on symptom improvement and tolerability.

CC.effect: Homozygous for the reference C allele. Normal dopamine transporter expression and standard response to methylphenidate and other stimulant medications expected.
CC.recommendation: Standard stimulant dosing per clinical guidelines. No pharmacogenomic-based dose adjustment needed.
